methods.description: This method describes the formation of bee colonies and the feeding experiment conducted to study the effects of different pollen and syrup diets on colony performance.

steps.description: Colonies were formed with packaged bees to have similar colonies for the majority of beekeepers.
steps.searchable_summary: form bee colonies with packaged bees

steps.description: Experimental groups were formed from 6 groups, including 8 colonies in each group.
steps.searchable_summary: create experimental groups with bee colonies

steps.description: Pollen samples from producers were stored in a deep freezer until use.
steps.searchable_summary: store pollen samples in deep freezer

steps.description: Pollens were moistened with sugar syrup and given to colonies in the form of meatballs.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and feed pollen meatballs to colonies

steps.description: Feeding was made at the same time and in the same amount.
The study was planned according to a Completely Randomized Design.
steps.searchable_summary: plan study using Completely Randomized Design

methods.description: This method outlines the statistical procedures used to analyze the data collected from the bee colony experiments, including ANOVA, Duncan test, Kruskal Wallis test, and survival analysis.

steps.description: One-way ANOVA was applied to determine the difference between the number of bee frames and the brood area measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA on bee frame and brood area data

steps.description: Multiple comparisons of the bee frame number, brood area averages and overwintering ability in the feeding groups were carried out using the Duncan test.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Duncan test for multiple comparisons

steps.description: The statistical difference between the pollen consumption preferences of honey bees and the pollen preferences according to time were analyzed with the Kruskal Wallis test.
steps.searchable_summary: use Kruskal Wallis test for pollen preference analysis

steps.description: The differences between the means of these groups were examined with the Dunn-Bonferroni multiple comparison test.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Dunn-Bonferroni test for mean differences

steps.description: The results of the longevity of bees were subjected to the Log-rank (Mantel-Cox) test of survival analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Log-rank test for survival analysis
